raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "At least 30% of all outdoor lighting in the U.S. is wasted, costing $3.3 billion and the release of 21 million tons of carbon dioxide per year.",
    "Increased light pollution has a disruptive impact on the circadian rhythms and migratory patterns of animals.",
    "70% of bird species migrate each year, and 80% of these migrate at night, navigating using the night sky.",
    "Excess artificial light attracts nocturnal-migratory birds, diverting them away from safe migration routes to human environments.",
    "Studies have demonstrated that light pollution can alter the behavior of wildlife, resulting in the death or decline of species such as turtles, birds, fish, reptiles, and insects.",
    "Artificial light at night can affect insect movement, foraging, and reproduction.",
    "When caught in a light plume of a light fixture, insects can circle around it until they die, or until the light is extinguished.",
    "Light pollution can interfere with natural circadian rhythms in humans.",
    "Circadian disruption in humans has been linked to sleep disorders, depression, cancer, and other adverse health conditions.",
    "Teens and adolescents living in high levels of artificial light at night are more likely to have mood and anxiety disorders and interrupted sleep patterns."
  ]
}
```
